mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in greater than 55% of rate-cut periods over a 3-year rolling walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in greater than 60% of instances when the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and a decline in the consumer price index indicates strong real demand without inflationary pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint retail sales and inflation signal yields negative 20-day returns in greater than 50% of occurrences over a 2-year rolling test.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns during recovery phases, with a Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5% signals a contraction in construction activity and downstream consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: Housing start declines greater than 5% are followed by positive 20-day returns in greater than 55% of cases during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined payroll and unemployment signal yields negative 5-day returns in greater than 50% of triggered instances over a 2-year rolling evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings and forces monetary policy tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPI acceleration is followed by positive 60-day returns in greater than 55% of instances during high inflation periods over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad economic expansion and rising corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns, with a Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.
